payload.summary: Add a model-neutral v1 invocation receipt validator and CLI command so real provider calls can be hash-bound to an authorized packet.
acceptance: A dependency-free validator accepts a fishtoucher.invocation/v1 object with exact top-level fields for invocation, run, packet, role, provider, model, transport, coordinator identity, request and response SHA-256 values, UTC start/end timestamps, status, and exit code.
The validator rejects unknown fields, blank identities, malformed SHA-256 values or timestamps, end-before-start ordering, and contradictions between success/failure status and exit code.
The receipt stores hashes and routing identity only; prompts, responses, credentials, and machine-specific secret locations are excluded.
The CLI validates one invocation receipt through a new invocation command.
Focused tests cover a valid DeepSeek receipt and adversarial structural and semantic failures.
requests: Acknowledge the packet before applying the DeepSeek-proposed patch.
Return exact changed files, commands, raw results, unresolved risks, and a skill-evolve decision.
payload.provider_note: This packet must be authored by the paid DeepSeek V4 Pro API through the registered Codex coordinator and secure adapter. The coordinator may gather context, apply DeepSeek's proposed patch, and run commands, but must not replace DeepSeek's implementation reasoning.
